narrative:
Markets are operating squarely in a business as usual regime, with a stress score of 21 out of 100 confirming a calm backdrop. Equity returns are modest and orderly, with SPY up 1.4% over five days at a z-score of 0.50, well within normal distributional range and showing no signs of either complacency or distress.

The VIX at 15.3 sits at just the 23rd percentile of its ten-year history, and the term structure is in normal contango, meaning the options market is pricing no near-term shock premium. Credit is strongly confirming this calm picture: HY spreads at 271 basis points are at only the 7th percentile of their five-year range, and IG at 72 basis points carries a z-score of negative 1.31, both indicating that credit markets are historically tight and fully aligned with the benign equity regime.

XLE stands out as the notable laggard, down 4.8% over five days, while EWZ and FXI each declined roughly 2 to 3 percent, pointing to some softness in energy and in Brazilian and Chinese equities specifically. Against that, technology led the way with XLK gaining 7.0%, broad emerging markets via EEM added 3.9%, and materials rounded out the winners, suggesting the weakness in specific geographies and energy is idiosyncratic rather than a broad risk-off signal.
